steps.text: Let's take two minutes to prepare for your meeting.
Find a comfortable seated position. Close your eyes or soften your gaze.
Breathe in for 4… hold for 4… out for 4… hold for 4.
Scan your body. Release tension in your shoulders, jaw, and hands.
Set one intention for this meeting. You are prepared and present.
You're about to step into the spotlight. Let's build your confidence.
Stand tall. Expand your body. Take up space. Hold for 30 seconds.
Picture yourself presenting with confidence. See the audience engaged. Hear your clear, steady voice.
Hum gently. Say 'I am ready' three times, louder each time.
Take a deep breath. You've got this. Walk in with power.
Difficult conversations are challenging. Let's approach with calm and clarity.
Breathe in for 4… hold for 6… out for 8.
Consider their viewpoint. What might they be feeling? What outcome do you both want?
Choose one word for how you want to show up: Patient? Clear? Compassionate?
Ground yourself. You can handle this with grace.
Let's clear the mental clutter in 90 seconds.
Shake out your hands. Roll your shoulders. Wiggle in your seat.
Big inhale through nose… Audible exhale through mouth. Again. Once more.
Imagine closing a browser tab. That meeting is over. Open a fresh, blank page.
One centering breath. You're ready for what's next.
That meeting is over. Let's release what you're carrying.
Breathe deeply. On exhale, drop your shoulders. Unclench your jaw. Relax your hands.
Gently roll your neck. Shrug shoulders up and drop. Side to side.
Breathe out stress you can't control. What's done is done.
Return to this moment. You're here now.
The workday is ending. Let's create a boundary between work and home.
Think of one thing you accomplished today. Something you're proud of, big or small.
Visualize closing your laptop. Work stays here. You're transitioning to personal time.
Breathe in calm. Breathe out work stress. You're shifting modes now.
Set one intention for your evening. You've earned this time.
Awareness of breathing, body sensations, transitioning from sleep.
Name three things you're grateful for. They can be small.
Look at your schedule with curiosity, not judgment. Notice what's ahead.
What's one thing you want to prioritize? How do you want to feel today?
Gentle stretches, shoulder rolls, standing poses, optional sun salutation.
Final breaths, affirmation - "I am ready. I am capable. I begin with intention."
The workday is over. Give yourself permission to let go.
Head to toe, noticing and releasing tension in each area.
What went well? What was challenging? No judgment, just notice.
Prompted questions, can write or think through.
Imagine stress leaving your body like sand through fingers.
4-7-8 breathing (4 in, 7 hold, 8 out), activates parasympathetic nervous system.
Arriving in the moment, letting go of week's momentum.
Guided questions about the week without judgment.
Choice of breath focus, body scan, or loving-kindness.
How would you talk to a friend who had your week? Offer yourself that kindness.
Light planning, hopes for the coming week (not work tasks).
Find your comfortable position. This is your sanctuary for the next 30 minutes.
Attention on natural breath, returning when mind wanders.
Choose your focus: breath, body scan, loving-kindness, open awareness, or visualization.
Gradually expand awareness. Notice sounds, sensations, the space around you.
Begin to return. Gentle movements. Carry this peace with you.
Close your eyes if comfortable.
In for 4... hold for 4... out for 4... hold for 4.
You are centered.
Lie down or sit comfortably. This is a journey through your body.
A few deep breaths to settle in.
Bring attention to your feet. Notice sensations in your toes, soles, heels.
Move up to your ankles, calves, knees, thighs. Release any tension.
Notice your hips, lower back. Let them sink into support.
Feel your belly rise and fall. Notice your chest, ribs.
Awareness to your fingertips, hands, forearms, upper arms.
Common tension areas. Soften your shoulders. Release your neck.
Relax your jaw, eyes, forehead. Let your face be soft.
Feel your entire body as one. A sense of wholeness and peace.
Slowly wiggle fingers and toes. Gentle movements to return.
You are renewed. Take your time opening your eyes.
